primary_sources.title: Federal Constitution Article 121 (Judicial Power); Courts of Judicature Act 1964
Indira Gandhi v Pengarah Jabatan Agama Islam Perak (2018) — Federal Court restated Article 121 judicial-power limits

notes: Apex court of Malaysia since 1985 (replacing the appeals to the UK Privy Council, abolished by Mahathir government). 1988 judicial crisis: Article 121(1) amended to remove 'judicial power' language; six judges suspended, two dismissed including Lord President Salleh Abas. Legitimacy partially restored post-2018 via Indira Gandhi ruling and Nik Elin (2024) on State List boundary.
